exam_title: Viewing Results for Warm Up, Exam #1 started on 2026-04-16 19:16:07

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements best describes a common test for assessing business objectives?

answers.explanation: Manageable is not a characteristic of well-written objectives
Remember the SMART acronym for defining measurable objectives. You may not be tested on this in your actual exam,but it's good to know this one
Realistic is not a characteristic of well-written objectives
Testable is not a characteristic of well-written objectives

questions.question_text: Becca is developing a data model that will be used to implement a relational database. What data model would Becca choose?

answers.explanation: Conceptual diagrams are independent of any solution or technology and are primarily used to establish a consistent vocabulary describing business information

answers.text: A logical or physical class diagram
answers.explanation: Class diagrams are used to support object-oriented software development vs. relational databases

answers.text: A logical or physical entity-relationship diagram
answers.explanation: Entity-Relationship Diagrams are the most common model for helping build relational databases

Conceptual diagrams are independent of any solution or technology and are primarily used to establish a consistent vocabulary describing business information. Class diagrams are used to support object-oriented software, which is not mentioned in the question

questions.question_text: What kind of diagram is used in the specify and model requirements task to show how processes or objects interact during a scenario by showing the information passed between them?

answers.explanation: A context diagram is a type of data flow diagram and is not used in the specify and model requirements task
An activity diagram is a type of process model, and is not used in the specify and model requirements task
This is the technique being described in this question and is used in the specify and model requirements task
A process diagram or model describes the flow of activities in a process. This is not a technique in the specify and model requirements task

questions.question_text: Medix International has hired you to work on a project to choose an Electronic Health Record (EHR) system. You are preparing an artifact to define measures of value attributes to be used for assessing and comparing solutions and alternative designs. Which of the following is NOT a consideration in creating this artifact?

answers.explanation: This is a listed consideration for assessment of a solution.
This not a consideration for acceptance and evaluation criteria
This is a listed consideration for assessment of a solution.
This refers to the value attributes the system should possess and maps directly into the evaluation of alternative solutions.

questions.question_text: A model is a descriptive and visual way to convey information to a specific audience in order to support analysis, communication, and understanding. What would a business analyst typically model?

answers.explanation: Needs can be modeled but when the focus of modeling is needs, the output is requirements
Solutions can be modeled but when the focus of modeling is the solution, the output is designs
Requirements are modeled when the focus is on understanding the need. Designs are modeled when the focus is on a solution
Needs are modeled in the form of requirements, and solutions are modeled in the form of designs

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT an element of business cases?

answers.explanation: This is a listed element.
Not mentioned in the BABOK
This is a listed element.
This is a listed element.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a consideration when approving requirements?

answers.explanation: BAs are responsible for ensuring that the stakeholders understand and accept the requirements as prerequisites to their approval
Conflict resolution and issue management are common occurrences while a BA is securing approvals
Part of defining the approval process is understanding stakeholders' roles
This is not one of the elements for approve requirements task

questions.question_text: You have completed the elicitation activity plan. You have prepared for and conducted a workshop to gather requirements. What is the typical next step that you would follow?

answers.text: Send meeting notes out to all attendees
answers.explanation: This is too general an answer as some level of meeting notes or minutes are typically distributed subsequent to any meeting. The question is looking for "capture the elicitation outcomes" which is an element of conduct elicitation
You might elect to do this but typically not until you have completed another step. The question is looking for "capture the elicitation outcomes" which is an element of conduct elicitation

answers.text: Record the results from the workshop so that they can be confirmed
answers.explanation: The BABOK actually uses the phrase "capture the elicitation outcomes." This is an element of conduct elicitation

answers.text: Confirm your elicitation results and communicate as appropriate
answers.explanation: You cannot confirm them yet; you're missing an essential step. The question is looking for "capture the elicitation outcomes" which is an element of conduct elicitation

questions.question_text: Which of the following best describes the difference between synchronous and asynchronous call message types in a sequence diagram?

answers.text: These terms refer to "signals" rather than message types. However, the signals in a synchronous call allows an object to receive messages in order from the sender.
answers.explanation: "Signal" is another name for an asynchronous call. The rest of the answer is made up

answers.text: A synchronous call transfers control to the receiving object; the sender cannot act until a return message is received. An asynchronous call allows the sending object to continue with its own processing while waiting for an answer.
A synchronous call can "synch" to many objects at a time allowing for multiple conversations; an asynchronous call is unable to "synch" meaning it is temporarily offline.
answers.explanation: A totally invented answer

answers.text: An asynchronous call transfers control to the receiving object; the sender cannot act until a return message is received. A synchronous call allows the sending object to continue with its own processing while waiting for an answer.
answers.explanation: Just the opposite

questions.question_text: How is the technique of stakeholder list, map, or persona used in the manage stakeholder collaboration task?

answers.text: Shows the informal relationships between stakeholders.
answers.explanation: This information is critical for successful collaboration and is just a portion of the information the tool would provide. Also included is knowing who is available to participate in BA work, and help in understanding which stakeholders should be consulted about different kinds of business analysis information

answers.text: Determines the appropriate roles for each stakeholder.
answers.explanation: This is how the tool could be used during the prepare for elicitation task

answers.text: This technique is not utilized in the manage stakeholder collaboration task.
answers.explanation: The stakeholder list, map, or persona technique is a suggested technique for this task

answers.text: Determines who should be consulted while preparing for elicitation.
answers.explanation: This is how the tool could be used during the prepare for elicitation task

questions.question_text: Amber is modeling use cases and has identified an actor in a use case that participates but has not initiated the use case. What is this actor known as?

answers.explanation: Primary and secondary actors are listed in the elements for Use Cases and Scenarios. "...actors who participate in the use case in a supporting role are called secondary actors."
There is no such term for an actor in a use case.
Primary infers the actor initiates.
There is no such term for an actor in a use case.

questions.question_text: After a long and challenging project, Roger has to determine if an enterprise is able to adapt to or effectively use the solution. What does Roger need to do in order to accomplish his goal?

answers.explanation: This only addresses a particular stakeholder group, not the enterprise
This is done to assess how the organization's structure is impacted by the solution
Two of the reasons this is done is to identify whether or not stakeholders understand the reasons why a solution exists and whether they view the solution as beneficial

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements best describes the purpose of the plan business analysis governance task?

answers.text: The purpose is to align with the project management governance approach.
answers.explanation: While this may be true in part, business analysis tasks may have differing requirements for governance than other aspects of the overall initiative

answers.text: The purpose is to define how decisions are made about requirements and designs, including reviews, change control, approvals, and prioritization.
answers.explanation: Decision rights is the primary component of governance

answers.text: The primary purpose is to provide inputs into the business analysis performance assessment report.
answers.explanation: Although this might logically ring true, it is not a listed input into the plan business analysis governance task

answers.text: The purpose is to provide oversight to business analysis activities and results.
answers.explanation: This is true in part but is not the best description of the purpose

answers.text: A predictive approach may indicate a more formal assessment of proposed changes.
answers.explanation: Predictive approaches focus on baselining requirements; once baselined, they can only be changed through the change control process

answers.text: Adaptive approaches try to minimize the impact of changes by utilizing iterative and incremental implementation techniques.
answers.explanation: Adaptive approaches welcome change by allowing the requirements list to be modified at will outside the boundaries of an iteration/sprint

answers.text: Many proposed changes may be withdrawn from consideration or declined before any formal approval is required.
answers.explanation: Stakeholders may change their mind about their change request and withdraw it from the process

answers.text: The project manager will determine the formality of the assessment process based on the information available, the apparent importance of the change, and the governance process.
answers.explanation: The business analyst determines these three components, not the project manager

questions.question_text: Nate is the senior business analyst for the NRO Company. He is working with Rod on completing the requirements prioritization of all the identified requirements. Why would Rod and Nate complete requirements prioritization?

answers.text: To determine which requirements carry the most risk.
answers.explanation: While risk may be a factor to be considered in prioritizing the requirement, it is not, by itself, a justification for completing the prioritization task

answers.text: To determine who created what requirement based on their position in the organization.
answers.explanation: This is not a justification for completing the prioritization task

answers.text: To determine which requirements should be completed first.
answers.explanation: This is a justification for completing the prioritization task

answers.text: To determine which requirements could be changed.
answers.explanation: This is not a justification for completing the prioritization task

questions.question_text: What are the guidelines and tools that are used for the Recommend Actions to Increase Solution Value task?

answers.text: Business objectives, change strategy, and risk analysis results
answers.explanation: These are guidelines and tools for the Assess Enterprise Limitations task

answers.text: Business objectives, current state description, and solution scope
Current state description and solution scope
answers.explanation: Business objectives is missing

answers.text: Decision analysis, organization modelling, and risk analysis and management
answers.explanation: These are techniques for this task

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a stated technique for the Define Requirements Architecture task?

answers.explanation: This is a stated technique for this task.
This is a stated technique for this task.
This is a stated technique for this task.

questions.question_text: What tool or system can provide significant benefits when there is a need to trace a large number of requirements and that may be too unwieldy with a manual approach?

answers.explanation: This is an invented term, there is no such system
Change management is a process, and there is no such thing as a change management traceability system
This is an invented term. There is no such system
A requirements management tool can provide a system for managing a large number of requirements. It can also act as a repository for all requirements

questions.question_text: A bank currently has a policy that a customer is limited to three ATM withdrawals a day with a cash limit of $600 total. This policy was identified as one of the root causes for customer dissatisfaction. How did the BA most likely identify this root cause?

answers.text: The BA conducted a workshop with customer service and used a fishbone diagram to identify the root cause of customer dissatisfaction.
answers.explanation: A fishbone diagram is a great way to capture potential causes, but not necessarily the root cause. It may also lead to a false trail or conclusion

answers.text: The BA elicited information through interviews with various customer service personnel to see what they thought the reasons were for customer dissatisfaction.
answers.explanation: This technique may or may not get to the root cause, because it relies on the questions asked and the customer service personnel's call experiences with the customers

answers.text: The BA sent out a survey to the customers asking them the about their source of their dissatisfaction.
answers.explanation: Rarely do all the surveys get returned and those that are returned may provide skewed data or the results are so low to be significant. It is also highly dependent on questions that get asked and how they were understood by the respondent

answers.text: The BA first reviewed all the data from the customer service call logs by data mining, then applied business rules analysis to identify the policy limiting the withdrawal amounts.
answers.explanation: Complaint data would first have to be mined and then the source could be identified through analysis of business rules. , 10.14, and 10.9

questions.question_text: Marie a new hire wants to understand roles, responsibilities, and reporting structures that exist in her organization. Which technique would help her achieve her goal?

answers.explanation: A RACI diagram is not a technique (and would not help her understand reporting structures).
This is a type of organizational model vs. a technique.
This is not a technique but, fundamental diagram used in organizational modeling

questions.question_text: Which of the following is the best definition of a "need" which is an input to many BA tasks?

answers.text: A need is synonymous with business requirements.
answers.explanation: The BACCM defines "need" as a problem or opportunity to be addressed. BABOK Table 2.1.1 The BABOK defines business requirements as statements of goals, objectives, and outcomes that describe why a change has been initiated

answers.text: A problem or opportunity to be addressed.
answers.explanation: The BACCM defines "need" as a problem or opportunity to be addressed. BABOK Table 2.1.1

answers.text: The perceived gap between business goals and the current state environment.
answers.explanation: The BACCM defines "need" as a problem or opportunity to be addressed. BABOK Table 2.1.1

answers.text: A need is synonymous with a requirement.
answers.explanation: The BACCM defines "need" as a problem or opportunity to be addressed. BABOK Table 2.1.1

questions.question_text: Gil was recently hired by Simon Technologies as a BA due to his extensive experience with Customer Relationship Management systems. Gil immediately began designing the future state processes based upon what he has seen work best for other organizations. According to the BACCM, what is Gil missing?

answers.explanation: Gil most likely has some idea of the value of the solution to the stakeholders, but he is lacking context. BABOK Table 2.1.1
Gil is working with the defined solution. BABOK Table 2.1.1

Context is defined by the BABOK as "everything relevant to the change that is within the environment." Gil is not conducting activities to understand Simon Technologies' environment. BABOK Table 2.1.1
Necessity is not a term utilized within the BACCM. BABOK TAble 2.1.1

questions.question_text: Stakeholders' attitudes can positively or negatively impact a change. It is therefore very important for the business analyst to develop and document a plan on how to effectively engage with each key stakeholder group. Which of the following work products would help document the plans?

answers.explanation: This documents communication considerations such as what needs to be communicated and when
As factors change during the life cycle of the initiative, the plan can be revisited and modified as appropriate to ensure the ongoing engagement of stakeholders
A power/interest grid is not covered in the BABOK
This is a guideline/tool used for assessment of stakeholder impact, not a work product

questions.question_text: Which of the following statements is NOT true regarding the benchmarking and market analysis technique?

answers.text: Market analysis can help determine if partnering, merging, or divesting are viable alternatives for an enterprise.
answers.explanation: Market analysis may be used to determine if partnering,merging, or divesting are viable alternatives for an enterprise

answers.text: Benchmarking identifies why similar companies are successful and what processes they used to become successful.
answers.explanation: This is one of the strengths listed in the BABOK

answers.text: Market analysis can be time-consuming and expensive, and the results may not be immediately available.
answers.explanation: This is one of the listed limitations in the BABOK

answers.text: Benchmarking produces innovative solutions that produce sustainable competitive advantage.
answers.explanation: This is not true. Benchmarking is not innovative.

questions.question_text: How is the solution core concept in the Business Analysis Core Concept Model applied in the requirements analysis and design definition knowledge area?

answers.text: The circumstances that influence and provide understanding of the change are considered when determine solution performance measures.
answers.explanation: This represents the context core concept. BABOK Table 7.0.1

answers.text: Potential value of the solution options are analyzed and quantified.
answers.explanation: This applies to the value core concept. BABOK Table 7.0.1

answers.text: The performance of the solution is examined and assessed to see if it is delivering the potential value.
answers.explanation: This is done in the solution evaluation knowledge area. BABOK Table 7.0.1 and Table 8.0.1.

answers.text: Potential solution options are defined and the one that is most likely to address the need and has the most value is recommended.
answers.explanation: This answer represents the solution core concept. BABOK Table 7.0.1
